testCases.title: Sending empty/spaces message is handled gracefully
steps: Send a message with only spaces
testCases.expectedResult: Bot either ignores the message or replies helpfully. Does NOT crash or go silent.
testCases.notes: Per PRD — any text is treated as a tracking code attempt.

testCases.title: Sending a very long string is handled gracefully
steps: Send a message with 500+ random characters to the bot
testCases.expectedResult: Bot replies with an error or not found message. Does NOT crash. Long string is NOT added as a tracked code.

testCases.title: CE Express API unavailable → user-friendly error shown
testCases.preconditions: Dev team can simulate API outage in staging (e.g. block the CE Express endpoint).
steps: Ask dev to simulate CE Express API being unavailable
Send a tracking code to the bot
testCases.expectedResult: Bot replies with a user-friendly error (e.g. 'Unable to fetch tracking info, please try again later.'). No raw error, no stack trace, no silence.
testCases.notes: Per PRD section 12 — error handling returns user-friendly messages when API is down.

testCases.title: Wrong command format is handled gracefully
steps: Send /delete with extra arguments: /delete code1 code2 code3
testCases.expectedResult: Bot handles gracefully — processes first code or replies with usage instructions. Does NOT crash.

testCases.title: State is preserved after bot restart
testCases.preconditions: User has at least 2 tracked codes. Dev restarts the bot service.
steps: Add 2 tracking codes and confirm with /list
Ask dev to restart the bot service
testCases.expectedResult: All previously tracked codes are still listed. State is preserved across restarts.
testCases.notes: Per PRD architecture — state persisted in /data/state.json via Docker volume. Critical for production.

testCases.title: Unknown command is handled gracefully
testCases.expectedResult: Bot either ignores it or suggests /help. Does NOT crash or show error trace.
